content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed all three pending amendments for planned-elsewhere reconciliation correctness. Amendment 1: Added extract_pe_bead_ids validation to reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings in success_reconciliation.rs. The function now loads the project record via FsProjectStore, resolves the prompt path via FileSystem::project_root, extracts the allowed PE bead IDs via task_prompt_contract::extract_pe_bead_ids, and skips any reconstructed mapping whose mapped_to_bead_id is not in the allowed set. Fail-closed: if the project record or prompt cannot be read, or the allowed set is empty, all PE-mapped reconstructions are blocked. Amendment 2: Moved PE validation before voter/arbiter prompts in final_review.rs. The allowed_pe_ids extraction and invalid mapped_to_bead_id stripping now runs on the canonical amendments list immediately after merge_final_review_amendments (line 610), before build_voter_prompt (line 659) and build_arbiter_prompt are called. This prevents hallucinated or out-of-scope bead IDs from biasing the voting panel with false 'no restart' promises. The existing validation on final_accepted_amendments is retained as defense-in-depth but reuses the earlier allowed_pe_ids binding instead of recomputing it. Amendment 3: Expanded the reconstruction dedup key in reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings from (active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id) to (active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id, completion_round). This allows later rounds to independently reconstruct the same finding/target pair — if round N's journal write succeeded but round N+1's failed, reconstruction can still recover N+1's mapping even though N's version exists in the journal. Updated the existing reconstruct_pe_mappings_deduplicates_by_round_latest_wins test to set up a project record and prompt file with the allowed PE bead IDs so the new validation does not block reconstruction in the test.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendment 1: Added FileSystem, FsProjectStore imports and ProjectStorePort, task_prompt_contract imports to success_reconciliation.rs. In reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings, added allowed PE set computation before the reconstruction loop: loads project record via FsProjectStore.read_project_record, resolves prompt path via FileSystem::project_root + record.prompt_reference, extracts allowed PE bead IDs via task_prompt_contract::extract_pe_bead_ids. Falls back to empty HashSet on any error (fail-closed).
2. [Completed] Amendment 1: Added validation check inside the reconstruction loop — after extracting mapped_to from each amendment, checks !allowed_pe_ids.contains(mapped_to) and continues (skips) if not in the allowed set. This runs before the dedup key check and journal write.
3. [Completed] Amendment 2: Changed let amendments = merge_final_review_amendments(...) to let mut amendments in final_review.rs (line 610). Added PE validation block immediately after the empty-amendments early return (line 642): extracts allowed_pe_ids via task_prompt_contract::extract_pe_bead_ids(&project_prompt), iterates amendments mutably, strips invalid mapped_to_bead_id values (sets to None when allowed set is empty or value not in set). This runs before build_voter_prompt (line 659) and build_arbiter_prompt.
4. [Completed] Amendment 2: Updated existing defense-in-depth validation on final_accepted_amendments (previously at line 1516) to reuse the earlier allowed_pe_ids binding instead of recomputing it. Updated the comment to clarify it is defense-in-depth against any code path that could re-introduce an invalid mapping.
5. [Completed] Amendment 3: Changed seen_keys HashSet type from (String, String, String) to (String, String, String, Option<u32>) in reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings. Updated the initial population from existing_mappings to include m.completion_round. Updated the identity_key construction inside the loop to include Some(round).
6. [Completed] Updated reconstruct_pe_mappings_deduplicates_by_round_latest_wins test to set up a project record (project.toml) and prompt file (prompt.md with '## Already Planned Elsewhere' section listing old-bead and new-bead) so the new PE validation allows reconstruction in the test.
7. [Completed] Fixed cargo fmt formatting issue: collapsed multi-line import to single line in test block.
8. [Completed] Ran cargo test (689 lib passed, 0 failed), cargo clippy (no new warnings from changed files), cargo fmt --check (clean).

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test --lib: 689 passed, 0 failed (up from 688+1 failed before fixes)
- cargo test reconstruct_pe_mappings_deduplicates_by_round_latest_wins: passes with project record and prompt setup
- cargo fmt --check: exit 0, no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: no new warnings from any changed file (success_reconciliation.rs, final_review.rs); all 19 warnings are pre-existing in unrelated files
- Amendment 1 verified: reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings now loads project record and prompt, extracts allowed PE IDs, and validates each mapped_to_bead_id before reconstruction. Empty allowed set (unreadable prompt or project record) blocks all PE reconstructions (fail-closed).
- Amendment 2 verified: PE validation on canonical amendments at line 643-654 (after merge, before voter/arbiter prompts). allowed_pe_ids reused at line 1527-1538 for defense-in-depth on final_accepted_amendments. Voter/arbiter prompts at build_voter_prompt and build_arbiter_prompt now only see validated PE mappings.
- Amendment 3 verified: seen_keys is now HashSet<(String, String, String, Option<u32>)> with completion_round in the key. identity_key includes Some(round). Two entries for the same (bead, finding, target) but different rounds are independent in the dedup set.
- git diff --stat HEAD -- src/: 2 files changed — success_reconciliation.rs (PE validation in reconstruction, 4-tuple dedup key, test setup), final_review.rs (early PE validation, defense-in-depth reuse)
- Files modified: src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs (imports, allowed PE set computation, validation check, 4-tuple dedup key, test project/prompt setup), src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs (mut amendments, early PE validation, defense-in-depth comment update)

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing clippy warnings (19 from code outside this change) cause cargo clippy -- -D warnings to fail; none are introduced by this change.
- The reconstruction PE validation in success_reconciliation.rs loads the project record and prompt on every reconciliation run. For the reconstruction path this adds one file read (project.toml) and one file read (prompt.md) per reconciliation. This is acceptable since reconstruction is rare (only when journal writes failed) and the files are small.
- If the prompt file format changes such that extract_pe_bead_ids fails to parse valid PE bead IDs, the empty allowed set will cause all reconstructed PE mappings to be skipped (fail-closed). This is the desired conservative behavior — false negatives (skipping valid PE mappings) trigger verification/restart, while false positives (accepting invalid PE mappings) would silently drop findings.
- The round-based dedup key expansion means that if reconstruction scans multiple rounds (hypothetical future change to reconstruct from more than just the latest round), the same finding/target pair from different rounds would produce separate entries. Currently only the latest round is scanned, so this is a no-op but future-proofs the dedup logic.
